steps.title: Acknowledge a shared trait
steps.content: The second sentence first admits that many dances share order, repetition, and synchronicity. Then "but" distinguishes the Madison by its extreme uniformity.

steps.title: Choose concession, not contrast
steps.content: A creates contrast too early, before the sentence's own "but." C would specify the previous sentence rather than concede a shared trait. D adds another supporting point instead of acknowledging a limitation. The answer is B.
